fields.value: GTM teams had to find the right documents across fragmented tools, web pages and drives
Users ask the GTM AI Assistant questions to retrieve knowledge and data, replacing manual document search and custom SQL or analyst support for complex analysis
GTM teams needed to find the right documents across fragmented tools, web pages and drives
At GA launch: 6 semantic views with 48 tables and ~1,400 columns; post-GA expanded to 10 semantic views with 64 tables and more than 1,750 columns

evidence.quote: In mid-2025, we launched the Snowflake GTM AI Assistant to roughly 6,000 users across our sales and marketing organization — our first full-scale enterprise deployment of Snowflake Intelligence.

Company
About Snowflake
Leadership & Board
Careers
Investor Relations
Trust Center
Brand Guidelines
Contact
Newsroom
Environmental, Social & Governance
Snowflake Ventures
End Data Disparity
Snowflake Summit 26
We started in late February 2025 with a narrow goal: build a RAG-based knowledge assistant so GTM teams could find the right documents across fragmented tools, web pages and drives.

The time savings varied by use case:
Minutes for simple knowledge or data retrieval
Hours for more complex analysis that would otherwise require custom SQL or analyst support

When we launched the AI assistant in mid-September 2025, it was powered by six semantic views with 48 tables and ~1,400 columns. After GA, we launched 40+ new features/data sources, expanding the data layer to 10 semantic views with 64 tables and more than 1,750 columns.
